title: CODEX Preparation of Reporter 96-Well Plates
author: Jerelyn Nick

steps.title: Prepare the Reporter Master Mix Aliquots for Each Cycle
steps.instruction: Prepare the Reporter Master Mix Aliquots for Each Cycle

steps.title: Label an amber microcentrifuge tube for each cycle
steps.instruction: Label an amber microcentrifuge tube for each cycle. Include tubes for the blank cycles at the beginning and end of the experiment.

steps.title: Aliquot Reporter Stock Solution into each...
steps.instruction: Aliquot Reporter Stock Solution into each tube following the chart below (volumes will vary depending on the number of reporters used in an individual cycle (see CODEX Cycle Template). %s %s Reporter Solutions: Label a 1.5 ml amber tube for each cyc %s %s le %s %s

steps.title: Add appropriate Reporters to the Reporter...
steps.instruction: Add appropriate Reporters to the Reporter Stock Solution aliquots to make a Master Mix for each cycle

steps.title: Briefly , Spin tubes using a bench top centrifuge
steps.instruction: Briefly , Spin tubes using a bench top centrifuge. Return Reporters to ice.

steps.title: column A based on number of...
steps.instruction: column A based on number of reporters in a particular cycle. steps.title: label any EMPTY channels Blank Cy7 Antibody (Reporter
steps.instruction: label any EMPTY channels Blank Cy7 Antibody (Reporter

steps.title: Prepare the 96-well Reporter Plate
steps.instruction: Prepare the 96-well Reporter Plate: Pipette 245 ul of Reporter containing Master Mix into the corresponding cycle well . Filled 96-well Reporter Plate with foil seal strips in place

steps.title: Use a different 96-well plate for each Reporter plate prepared
steps.instruction: Use a different 96-well plate for each Reporter plate prepared. Lightly cover entire rows of wells (i.e. B1-B12) with strips of foil seal. Keep the filled 96-well plates IN THE DARK to avoid photo bleaching. Store at 4˚C for up to two weeks. 4 °C
